channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Utah HC shuts out Minnesota for third straight victory
item.description: ST. PAUL (AP) - Karel Vejmelka made 26 saves for his first shutout of the season, and the Utah Hockey Club defeated the Minnesota Wild 4-0 at Xcel Energy Center on Thursday. “We did a great job,” Vejmelka said. “Boys played real well in front of me and they helped me a lot.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Nikola Jokic hits 66-foot heave to highlight his 5th straight triple-double
item.description: Nikola Jokic took an inbounds pass with 1.7 seconds left in the third quarter, turned and let fly with a 66-foot heave that got all net at the other end.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kelly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ovechkin moves within 20 goals of breaking Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record, Capitals beat Kraken 3-0
item.description: Alex Ovechkin moved within 20 goals of breaking Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record with an empty-netter, helping the Washington Capitals beat the Seattle Kraken 3-0 on Thursday night for their sixth straight victory.
item.dc:creator: Allyson Ballard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An injured Novak Djokovic stops in the Australian Open semifinals against Alexander Zverev
item.description: An injured Novak Djokovic has quit after dropping the first set of his Australian Open semifinal against Alexander Zverev.
item.dc:creator: Howard Fendrich, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alysa Liu returns from 2-year absence and leads after short program at US Championships
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — Alysa Liu wore the smudge of blood on her dress at the U.S. Figure Skating Championships on Thursday night like a poignant badge of honor, symbolic of everything she has gone through over the past few years. The 19-year-old has been through some battles.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Recording captured ex-interpreter impersonating Ohtani to transfer $200,000, prosecutors say
item.description: A nearly four-minute audio recording allegedly captured Shohei Ohtani 's former interpreter Ippei Mizuhara impersonating the baseball star on a call with a bank as he attempted to transfer $200,000 for what he describes as a car loan, federal prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ludvig Aberg, Lanto Griffin share lead after fierce coastal wind interrupts Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Ludvig Aberg and Lanto Griffin shared the lead Thursday at Torrey Pines in the Farmers Insurance Open after ferocious wind forced an 86-minute delay and prevented dozens of players from finishing the second round.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tom Brady compares Commanders rookie Jayden Daniels to legendary golfer
item.description: NFL legend Tom Brady compared Washington Commanders quarterback Jayden Daniels to a young Tiger Woods after the rookie led his team to a win over the Detroit Lions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aggies make 16 three-pointers in bounce-back win
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Following its first road loss of the season, the Utah State basketball team shot the lights out at the Spectrum in a 90-69 victory over Nevada Wednesday night. Ian Martinez led the way with 23 points, making 7 of 9 from beyond the arc. Utah State was 16-of-30 (.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Runnin' Utes crushed by #7 Houston, 70-36
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Riding a three-game winning streak, the Runnin' Utes had gained some confidence the last couple of weeks. But Wednesday night against #7 Houston, the Utes came crashing back down to Earth. Milos Uzan scored 14 points, L.J. Cryer added 13 points and No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Students receive career lessons at Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: About 30 local students got more than a chance to watch golf at Torrey Pines on Wednesday — they got valuable career insights that could shape their futures.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, Fox 5

item.title: PGA Tour looking to move 2025 Genesis Invitational to Torrey Pines
item.description: The tournament was scheduled to take place from Feb. 13 to 16 at The Riviera Country Club, which is in the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card
item.description: Topps, the card manufacturer, confirmed that the Paul Skenes 1/1 Debut Patch card was in fact unpacked.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles 11-year-old pulls one-of-a-kind baseball card
item.description: An 11-year-old baseball card collector from Los Angeles just pulled what is likely the most important card they will ever pull. Topps, the card manufacturer, confirmed Tuesday morning that the Paul Skenes 1/1 Debut Patch card was in fact unpacked. The card features the patch that the 2023 No.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Utah Hockey Club at Rice Eccles Stadium? It could happen
item.description: Could Utah Hockey Club play a game outdoors at Rice Eccles Stadium? Owner Ryan Smith indicated it could be a possibility sometime in the future.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks take losing streak into game against the Golden Knights
item.description: Vegas Golden Knights (29-13-3, in the Pacific Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (14-28-3, in the Central Division) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-49er Dana Stubblefield to remain in prison for now after judge denies bail ruling
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Former San Francisco 49er Dana Stubblefield, whose 2020 rape conviction was vacated late last year, will remain in California state prison for now after a judge declined to make a bail ruling Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
